role: The src directory serves as the main source code root for the ArchSpine mirror system, housing all core logic, infrastructure, services, tasks, types, and utilities that implement the system's architectural awareness and repository mirroring capabilities.
responsibility: Collectively, the components in the src directory provide the complete implementation of the ArchSpine system: from asset templates and AST extraction to CLI orchestration, core execution infrastructure, engine modules for scanning/dependency analysis/rule enforcement/fix generation, a comprehensive infrastructure layer (database, configuration, LLM orchestration, MCP integration), service orchestration for all workflows (sync, check, fix, init, publish), pipeline task stages, canonical type definitions, and essential utilities for CLI presentation, filesystem operations, and agent instruction synchronization. Together, they enable ArchSpine to establish and maintain a structured mirror of a repository's architectural state, support AI agent interaction, and enforce governance rules.

children.role: Root directory for the ArchSpine architectural awareness system, containing the agent entry skill and template artifacts for identity, prompts, rules, and external documentation.
AST analysis layer for the ArchSpine mirror system that provides language-specific parsing, symbol extraction, and language discovery capabilities.
Entry point and utility layer for the ArchSpine command-line interface, containing the command dispatcher, formatting helpers, and subcommand handler modules.
Foundational execution infrastructure for the ArchSpine pipeline, providing configuration validation, error handling, task state management, and an abstract task lifecycle.
Engine modules that implement core ArchSpine functionality including scanning, dependency analysis, rule enforcement, fix generation, and reporting.

Infrastructure directory that contains the core supporting subsystems for the ArchSpine mirror system, including database, configuration, LLM orchestration, credential management, file system operations, and MCP integration.
Orchestration and service layer for the ArchSpine mirror system, providing all core workflows (sync, check, fix, init, publish, administration) and runtime lifecycle management.
Pipeline stage tasks for the ArchSpine mirror system that implement a sequence of processing stages from source discovery to artifact generation.
Core type definitions for the ArchSpine mirror system, covering protocol schemas and view output interfaces.

Core utilities and infrastructure modules for ArchSpine CLI, covering CLI presentation, filesystem operations, user prompts, git hook management, locking, path normalization, and agent instruction synchronization.
